mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production indicate accelerating supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a mean forward return below zero and a t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in the consumer price index erode real corporate earnings and trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between CPIAUCSL growth and 20-day forward returns remains positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) across all rolling 2-year test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200 thousand signal robust economic expansion and strong corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 60 percent of months where PAYEMS exceeds 200 thousand, or the signal's information coefficient falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with a declining federal funds rate creates a favorable macroeconomic environment that boosts equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 growth and FEDFUNDS changes yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day returns over any 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling INDPRO and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 5-year backtest.
